name: passdetective
title: passdetective

commands.name: PassDetective
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# PassDetective -h
 The "extract" command allows you to automatically extract passwords from shell history change descriptions.
 By analyzing the history of shell commands, this tool can identify and extract passwords that were used during
 previous commands and display them for further inspection or use.
 
 Usage:
 PassDetective [flags]
 PassDetective [command]
 
 Available Commands:
 completion  Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
 extract     Extract passwords from shell history
 help        Help about any command
 
 Flags:
 -h, --help   Help message for PassDetective
 
 Use "PassDetective [command] --help" for more information about a command.

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=08feffefbd9b538834c6a58b083290acc250446b, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
CLI tool that scans shell command history
This package contains a command-line tool that scans the shell command
history for mistakenly written passwords, API keys, and secrets. It uses
regular expressions to identify potential sensitive information and helps
avoid accidentally exposing sensitive data in the command history.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use PassDetective because ### passdetective.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair PassDetective with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `PassDetective --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### passdetective.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install passdetective
Install with: sudo apt install passdetective
